title: CSS Fonts
content:
### CSS Fonts

Font properties control the appearance of text characters.

---

### Font Family

```css
p {
  font-family: Arial, Helvetica, sans-serif;
}
```

List fallback fonts; browser uses the first available.

---

### Generic Font Families

| Family | Looks Like | Example |
|--------|-----------|---------|
| `serif` | Small lines at ends | Times New Roman |
| `sans-serif` | Clean, no lines | Arial, Helvetica |
| `monospace` | Equal width letters | Courier New |
| `cursive` | Handwriting style | Brush Script MT |
| `fantasy` | Decorative | Impact |

Always end `font-family` with a generic family as fallback:

```css
font-family: "Times New Roman", Times, serif;
```

---

### Font Size

```css
font-size: 16px;       /* Fixed pixels */
font-size: 1.5rem;     /* Relative to root */
font-size: 1.2em;      /* Relative to parent */
font-size: 80%;        /* Percentage */
font-size: large;      /* Keyword */
font-size: 100vw;      /* Viewport width */
```

---

### Font Weight

```css
font-weight: normal;    /* 400 */
font-weight: bold;      /* 700 */
font-weight: 100;       /* Thin */
font-weight: 300;       /* Light */
font-weight: 600;       /* Semi-bold */
font-weight: 900;       /* Black */
```

---

### Font Style

```css
font-style: normal;
font-style: italic;
font-style: oblique;
```

---

### Font Variant

```css
font-variant: normal;
font-variant: small-caps; /* Lowercase becomes small caps */
```

---

### Font Shorthand

```css
/* style weight size/line-height family */
font: italic bold 16px/1.5 Arial, sans-serif;
```

**Required:** `size` and `family`. Order matters.

---

> **Best Practice:** Use `rem` for font sizes to respect user's browser settings.
codeExample:
Serif font (Georgia)

Sans-serif font (Arial)

Monospace font (Courier New)

Italic text
